title: eriocoma parishii subsp. depauperata
intro: Eriocoma parishii subsp. depauperata, commonly known as a type of grass or sedge, is a plant species that belongs to the family Restionaceae. The plant is known to have medicinal properties, particularly for its use in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include relief from coughs, colds, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing. Eriocoma parishii subsp. depauperata can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
